record.field: OpenProject is an open-source project management platform for classic, agile and hybrid teams: roadmaps, Gantt charts, boards and time tracking, fully self-hosted.

OpenProject is an open-source project management platform for classic, agile and hybrid teams: roadmaps, Gantt charts, boards and time tracking, fully self-hosted.

OpenProject brings project planning, task management, agile boards, roadmaps, bug tracking and team collaboration into a single self-hosted application. Built on Ruby on Rails and backed by PostgreSQL, it targets companies that want full ownership of their project data and a privacy-friendly alternative to Jira, Trello, Asana or MS Project. Work is organized into projects and work packages, so engineering, product and PMO teams can plan, track and report on cross-functional initiatives from one place.

Key features
  • Work packages with statuses, types, custom fields and hierarchical parent/child tasks
  • Gantt charts and roadmaps for classic project planning and milestones
  • Agile boards for Scrum and Kanban with backlogs, sprints and story points
  • Time and cost tracking, budgets and cost reporting
  • Wikis, forums, document management and per-project role-based permissions
  • REST API, LDAP/SAML/OIDC authentication and email notifications
Best for

Companies and teams that want a self-hosted alternative to Jira, Trello, Asana or MS Project: engineering, product and PMO teams managing roadmaps, sprints and cross-functional work with full data ownership. Plan for at least 4 GB of RAM for comfortable use.

services: openproject: image: openproject/openproject:15 restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' environment: OPENPROJECT_HOST__NAME: '{{ domain }}' OPENPROJECT_HTTPS: 'true' OPENPROJECT_SECRET_KEY_BASE: '{{ secret_key_base }}' OPENPROJECT_RAILS__RELATIVE__URL__ROOT: '' DATABASE_URL: 'postgres://openproject:{{ db_password }}@db:5432/openproject?pool=20&encoding=unicode&reconnect=true' volumes: - ./assets:/var/openproject/assets depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: postgres:16 restart: unless-stopped environment: POSTGRES_DB: openproject POSTGRES_USER: openproject POSTGRES_PASSWORD: '{{ db_password }}' healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U $${POSTGRES_USER} -d $${POSTGRES_DB} interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
PostgreSQL password for the OpenProject database user.
Rails secret key base used to sign sessions and cookies.
